text: The patient is positioned in a prone jackknife position on the operating table, with visible botox taped aside for the procedure.
The external opening of the anal fistula is visible, showing the area where the fistula tract begins on the skin surface.
The surgeon injects normal saline through the external opening of the anal fistula to assess the presence of an internal opening.
The surgeon probes the fistula tract, inserting a probe into the external opening to explore the path of the fistula.
The surgeon curates the fistula tract, visually inspecting and manipulating the tissue to address the fistula's pathway.
The surgeon soaks the fistula plug in normal saline, preparing it for insertion into the fistula tract.
The surgeon inserts the fistula plug into the external opening of the fistula tract, following preparation steps including soaking the plug in normal saline.
The surgeon closes the internal opening of the fistula using a 3-0 Vicryl suture, securing the closure with precise stitching.
The surgeon adjusts the length of the fistula plug to match the length of the fistula tract, ensuring a proper fit within the tract.
